rationale: The triggering process (A0) uses a Right-to-Left Override (RTLO/U+202E) character in its filename — 'cod.3aka3.scr' renders visually as something like 'rcs.3aka3.doc' to deceive users — a well-known masquerading technique for malicious executables disguised as documents. The file resides in C:\ProgramData\victim\ and was launched by explorer.exe (user double-click), indicating social engineering delivery. Within seconds of execution, the same process initiated an outbound TCP connection to 192.168.0.5:1234 (EV-001), a non-standard port strongly suggestive of C2 callback. The process then spawned cmd.exe (EV-002), which in turn spawned powershell.exe (EV-003), forming a classic execution chain (SCR → cmd → PowerShell) consistent with post-exploitation activity. The combination of RTLO filename spoofing, immediate C2 network connection, and shell/PowerShell spawning constitutes a high-confidence malicious verdict; no benign explanation accounts for all four indicators together.
